stories.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
stories.summary: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."

stories.title: Israeli foreign minister to ‘sever all contact’ with top EU diplomat
stories.summary: Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Sa’ar vowed on Thursday to cut contact with top EU diplomat Kaja Kallas, triggering a public spat that Kallas later tried — and failed — to defuse. “Ms. Kaja Kallas … has for some time now been acting obsessively and with blatant unfairness toward the State of Israel,” Sa’ar wrote on X Thursday morning, referring to reports last month that Kallas had compared Israel’s treatment of Palestinians to apartheid-era South Africa. Sa’ar accused the EU’s

stories.title: Magyar and Metsola discuss EU dropping rule of law procedure against Hungary
stories.summary: Péter Magyar said Thursday that he and European Parliament President Roberta Metsola discussed the EU dropping its rule-of-law disciplinary procedure against Hungary. “We agreed that the Article 7 procedure launched against Hungary eight years ago — in response to concerns over the rule of law and democracy under the previous government — should be concluded by autumn,” the Hungarian prime minister wrote on X after a meeting with Metsola in Brussels. Magyar, a former MEP, said he planned to addr

stories.title: Gesundheitsreform: Die Pharmaindustrie und der Streit ums Sparen
stories.summary: Kann man Deutschland zum weltweit führenden Pharma-Standort machen und gleichzeitig eine Gesundheitsreform auf den Weg bringen? Wir blicken auf das Spannungsfeld zwischen der künftigen Finanzierung der gesetzlichen Krankenkassen und der Wettbewerbsfähigkeit Deutschlands. Zu Gast im Policy Talk ist Heidrun Irschik-Hadjieff. Sie ist die Geschäftsführerin der deutschen Tochtergesellschaft des Pharmakonzerns Sanofi. Im Gespräch mit POLITICO-Gesundheitsexpertin Marie Zahout rechnet sie mit dem geplan

stories.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the wo

stories.title: Gas prices fall below $4 on average after Trump’s signing of Iran deal to end war
stories.summary: Drivers feel some relief but prices still a dollar more per gallon overall since before US-Israel attack on Iran Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The average price of US gasoline fell to just under $4 a gallon on Thursday for the first time since March , following the announcement of a preliminary agreement between the US and Iran to end the war and reopen the strait of Hormuz. The development has provided some relief to drivers who have seen soaring costs amid Washington’s war

stories.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
stories.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

stories.title: Germany's foreign minister: 'European unity is decisive'
stories.summary: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul, in an interview with DW, underscores the trans-Atlantic partnership, close cooperation with Poland, continued support for Ukraine and European unity.

stories.title: A gyereke is végignézte, ahogy ordítva ütött ököllel, rugdosott egy másik autót egy budapesti férfi
stories.summary: Az eset még 2024-ben történt, előtte az ordítozó autós egy másik sofőrrel versenyzett az M2-es autóúton Göd irányába.

stories.title: Diplomáciai nagyüzem vár Magyar Péterre a két napra nyúló első EU-csúcsán
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök egy sor találkozóval alapoz a csúcs előtt, amin Zelenszkij is részt vehet. Ukrajna, a közel-keleti helyzet és a hétéves költségvetés a témák között.

stories.title: Az EP elnöke egyetértett Magyarral, hogy őszig le kell zárni a 7-es cikkes eljárást
stories.summary: Ezzel a lendülettel meg is hívta Roberta Metsolát Magyarországra.

stories.title: Hat nap alatt tekerné le egy Down-szindrómával élő magyar kisfiú az apjával a 700 kilométeres Tour Horizont futamot
stories.summary: Nekem is kérdés még, hogy fogja bírni Artúr, de a korábbi hosszabb túrák alapján bízom benne, hogy nem viseli meg majd őt a futam – mondta a kisfiú apja, Hegedűs Kornél.

stories.title: A politikailag jól ismert szereplőket szűrné ki a kormány a dohány- és kaszinókoncessziókból
stories.summary: A Magyarországon működő közel 5800 dohánybolt ellátásáért egy nagy hódmezővásárhelyi cég felel. Tulajdonosai a cég alapításának első 10 évében 52,5 milliárd forintot vettek fel osztalékként.

stories.title: Kovács István játékvezető különleges mezben mutatkozik be, övé az ezredik vb-meccs
stories.summary: A FIFA-bírófőnök, Pierluigi Collina a romániai játékvezetőnek adta a megtiszteltetést, hogy a történelem része legyen.

stories.title: Kifulladt a növekedés, csúsznak le a magyar webshopok, a Temu tarol az e-kereskedelemben
stories.summary: Sokkal gyorsabban nő a Temu, mint a piacméret, vagyis a versenytársak elől viszi el a magyar vásárlókat.

stories.title: Málna alig, fekete ribizli talán egyáltalán nem terem a jövőben Magyarországon
stories.summary: A magyar málna-, ribizli- és szedertermelés is óriási kihívásokkal küzd, a nyolcvanas évekhez képest 90 százalékkal kevesebb málnásunk van. A vegyszerezett import pedig kevesebb ízt és tápanyagot tartalmaz.

stories.title: Karácsony Gergely kéri vissza a Városligetet Budapestnek
stories.summary: A főpolgármester arra reagált, hogy a kormány kimondta: nem épül új épület a parkban.

stories.title: A Fidesz-kampányt szervező alapítványtól igazolt új kurátort a Transzparens Újságírásért Alapítvány. Aztán úgy tűnt, hogy megszűnt
stories.summary: Mondhatnánk, hogy az új igazolással megkérdőjeleződött az újságírás átláthatóságáért küzdő alapítvány függetlensége, de nem szeretnénk ilyesmivel nevetségessé tenni magunkat. Közben az alapítvány olyan transzparens lett, hogy teljesen el is tűnt: a honlapja elérhetetlenné vált, és az emailcíme is megszűnt. Előbbi viszont azóta vissza is tért.

stories.title: Csehország–Dél-afrikai Köztársaság 1-1
Molnár Zsolt kilép az MSZP-ből
stories.summary: Az óbudai korrupciós ügy egyik kulcsfigurája azzal indokolta a döntését, hogy nem szeretné, hogy az ügy személyes vagy politikai terhet jelentsen a Magyar Szocialista Párt számára.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Nem belpolitikai, pártpolitikai okokból fogunk akár egyetérteni, akár vétózni
stories.summary: Az EU-csúcs előtt a V4-es tárgyalásáról, a brüsszeli fogadtatásáról és a napi egymillió eurós büntetésről is beszélt a kormányfő.

stories.title: A V4-ek vezetőivel posztolt képet Magyar Péter, és azt írta, újraindítják a Visegrádi Együttműködést
stories.summary: Azt is megerősítette, hogy június 23-án érkezik Budapestre és Gödöllőre a lengyel, a cseh és a szlovák miniszterelnök.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Brüsszelben: A határidőt nem rajtunk kellene számonkérni, mi most kármentésben vagyunk
stories.summary: A Magyarországot sújtó napi egymillió eurós bírságról azt mondta, hogy dolgoznak a megoldáson, de nem tudta megmondani, mikorra mentesülhet az ország a menekültügyi szabályozás miatt kiszabott büntetés alól.

stories.title: Osztrák kancellár az első EU-csúcson Magyar Péterrel: Magyarország egy Európa-párti utat választott
stories.summary: Andrej Plenković horvát miniszterelnök uniós ügyekben konstruktív megközelítésre, a kétoldalú kapcsolatokban új szakaszra számít Magyar Pétertől.

stories.title: Az erőtlen csehek Dél-Afrikával sem bírtak, 1-1-re végeztek
stories.summary: Ezzel a döntetlennel jó eséllyel mindkét csapat búcsúzik a vb-től.

stories.title: Magyar: "We will represent Hungary differently"
stories.summary: Ahead of his first EUCO summit, the Hungarian PM spoke about how he intends to represent Hungary at the EU, how he was welcomed in Brussels, and the upcoming meeting of the V4 in Hungary.

stories.title: Októberig kapott haladékot Budapest a szolidaritási hozzájárulásra, de akkor 86 milliárdot kell majd fizetnie
stories.summary: A Fővárosi Önkormányzat szerint a kormány megértette, hogy egy újabb inkasszó bármely időpontban fizetésképtelenségbe sodorhatná Budapestet.

stories.title: Lássuk meg, mit lehet tenni – mondta az EP-elnök az uniós ezermilliárdokat érintő perről
stories.summary: A képviselőkkel egyeztetve fognak lépni az igazságügyi csomag miatti perben és a hetes cikkes eljárásban is, közölte a Telex kérdésére Roberta Metsola, miután Magyar Péterrel tárgyalt.

stories.title: 444: Költségvetési csalás gyanúja miatt nyomoznak a MOL – Új Európa Alapítvánnyal szemben
stories.summary: Felmerült, hogy az alapítvány szabálytalan szerződéseken keresztül sokszor százmillió forintnyi adót nem fizetett meg a magyar államnak.

stories.title: Sierra Leone’s first lady refuses to condemn FGM without ‘reliable data’ on harms
stories.summary: Exclusive: health professionals, survivors and politicians voice concerns in open letter over comments by Fatima Maada Bio, who denies supporting the practice The first lady of Sierra Leone has denied that she supports female genital mutilation amid rising anger around her perceived approval of the practice. But in an exclusive response to the Guardian, Fatima Maada Bio, the wife of President Julius Maada Bio, also said she would not openly condemn FGM until she saw “reliable data” that the prac

stories.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.title: The Guardian view on defending Europe in a new era: collaboration is the key | Editorial
stories.summary: The recent abandonment of plans for a Franco-German fighter jet sent a disastrous signal. Strategic autonomy will be jointly achieved or not at all It has become a truism to assert that Europe needs to fast-track its own strategic independence in a volatile world. A recent paper from the European Council on Foreign Relations describes the continent’s leaders as grappling with “a ‘Schrödinger’s NATO’ moment, in which America remains formally inside the alliance while behaving as though it were no

stories.title: How Germany and Poland rebooted relations 35 years ago
stories.summary: When Germany and Poland signed the Treaty on Good Neighborliness, Friendship and Cooperation in June 1991, they chose partnership over enmity and became close NATO and EU allies. But it hasn't all been plain sailing.

stories.title: EU, Ukraine start formal accession talks after Orban delay
stories.summary: The EU has formally opened the first stage of accession negotiations with Ukraine. This comes two years later than planned, as former Hungarian PM Viktor Orban was blocking the process prior to his election defeat.

stories.title: Moscow oil refinery struck in Ukraine’s biggest air raid on city since start of war
stories.summary: Kyiv says attack, which also forced evacuation at Russia’s biggest airport, was in response to strike on historic monastery Explainer: how significant was attack on Moscow? Ukrainian drones have hit several locations across Moscow in Kyiv’s biggest air raid on the city since the start of Russia’s full-scale invasion, setting a major ⁠oil refinery on fire and forcing evacuations at the country’s largest airport. Volodymyr Zelenskyy described the attack as a response to Russia’s strike on a histor

stories.title: Régulation de l’IA : les pays du G7 tentent de s’entendre face à la Chine
stories.summary: EVIAN-LES-BAINS — Réunis depuis lundi à Evian-les-Bains à l’occasion du G7, les pays occidentaux tentent de trouver des points de convergence sur la régulation de l’IA, en pleine concurrence avec la Chine. A l’invitation d’Emmanuel Macron, une dizaine de leaders de la tech — parmi lesquels Demis Hassabis (Google Deepmind), Arthur Mensch (Mistral AI), Sam Altman (OpenAI), ou Dario Amodei (Anthropic) — se sont ainsi retrouvés mercredi pour un déjeuner de travail sur le numérique. Au me

stories.title: Ukrainian drones hit oil refinery and residential building in Moscow – video
stories.summary: Flames and plumes of smoke filled the sky after a Moscow oil refinery was stuck in a Ukrainian drone attack. Up to 180 drones that were purportedly headed towards the Russian capital were shot down by air defence forces, according to Moscow's mayor. A high-rise residential building near the refinery was also damaged in the attack Moscow oil refinery on fire after ‘large-scale’ Ukrainian drone strike Europe live – latest updates Continue reading...

stories.title: Moment fuel storage lid flies off Moscow refinery during drone attack – video
stories.summary: Footage shows the moment an explosion at an oil refinery in Moscow caused a fuel storage lid to fly off. The explosion was caused by a Ukrainian drone attack where one of Moscow’s most important energy facilities was damaged for the second time this week Moscow oil refinery struck in Ukraine’s biggest air raid on city since start of war Continue reading...

stories.title: Rightwing MEPs chant 'send them back' after controversial deportation plans are passed – video
stories.summary: Several lawmakers celebrated a vote in the European parliament approving plans to increase deportations of undocumented people. Chants of 'send them back' spurred other MEPs to chant back 'shame on you'. The plans could lead to ICE-style immigration measures being implemented in Europe, which have been criticised by rights groups Continue reading...

stories.title: Anger at ‘send them back’ chants by rightwing MEPs after EU migration law vote
stories.summary: Other lawmakers respond with ‘shame on you’ in heated confrontation over passing of plan to increase deportations Rightwing MEPs have come under fire after they celebrated a vote aimed at increasing deportations across the EU with chants of “send them back”, leading other lawmakers to respond with cries of “shame on you”. The heated confrontation in the European parliament came on Wednesday after lawmakers voted 418 to 218 to approve controversial measures aimed at increasing deportations of und

stories.title: ‘If Ukraine burns, your Moscow will burn,’ Zelenskyy warns after overnight strikes in Russia – as it happened
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president ramps up rhetoric after overnight drone strikes on Russian capital Hegseth makes it clear that the review will not be just a box-ticking exercise. “ It’s a review that some countries will fail and others will pass with flying colours. In the end, the review is intended to both improve US force posture and basing and strengthen Nato 3.0.” “It will be designed to ensure that Nato is moving fast and irreversibly toward Europe leading , stepping up to take primary responsibility

stories.title: What did Ukraine target in Moscow and how significant was the drone attack?
stories.summary: Mass drone strike on capital brought the war to Russians, but Ukrainians will be braced for Kremlin’s response Ukraine hit Moscow with nearly 200 drones in its largest ever attack on the Russian capital on Thursday, striking an oil refinery and sending huge plumes of smoke billowing over the city’s south. The towering columns of smoke rising above Moscow offered a stark demonstration of Ukraine’s growing ability to strike deep inside Russia with its increasingly sophisticated, largely domestical

stories.title: UK attorney general tells staff to stop using X amid disinformation concerns
stories.summary: Exclusive: Richard Hermer’s department understood to be first in government to restrict use after recent riots The attorney general has told his office to no longer post on X, making it the first UK government department to stop using the Elon Musk-owned platform amid increasing worries about its use to incite violence and racism. Richard Hermer’s office last posted on X on Friday, and it is understood that officials have been told to no longer use the site, unless for the specific purpose of co

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump says US expects ‘complete ceasefire on all fronts, including Lebanon, Hezbollah and Israel’
stories.summary: US president says he encourages Middle East countries to ‘maintain commitment to allowing our negotiations to beautifully unfold’ Reaction: Donald Trump’s Iran deal met with anger, relief and incredulity Analysis: Trump’s Iran deal is result of unrealistic ambitions for an untenable war Donald Trump had urged Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu to “stop blowing up buildings” during a phone call about Israel’s military campaign in Lebanon, according to a report in the Wall Street Journal. T

stories.title: Moscow uses 'Russian Houses' in Africa to lure recruits into war in Ukraine, investigation shows
stories.summary: Ukrainian military intelligence has revealed that Russia's expanding network of "Russian Houses" functions as a recruitment pipeline for its war, luring young Africans with promises of education and jobs before some are sent to the front lines or into drone factories.

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: Hegseth launches six-month review of US force posture in Europe
stories.summary: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced a six-month review of America’s military footprint in Europe on Thursday, escalating the Trump administration’s push on NATO allies to take greater responsibility for the continent’s defense. The review will examine U.S. force posture and basing across Europe, including troop levels, military access, basing rights and overflight permissions. Hegseth, speaking at a NATO meeting of defense ministers in Brussels, cast the effort as a test of whether all

stories.title: Australian net overseas migration falls to lowest level since 2022 – but the Coalition says that’s still too high
stories.summary: Australian Bureau of Statistics figures show migration growth is above pre-pandemic levels but tracking steadily down Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Net overseas migration added 301,000 people to Australia’s population last year, the lowest increase since mid-2022 but still above the pre-pandemic pace. The new figures from the Australian Bureau of Statistics come amid an increasingly fraught political debate around immigration, following Pauline Hanson’s declaration

stories.title: California ‘billionaire tax’ makes ballot despite opposition from tech moguls
stories.summary: Wealth tax criticized by billionaires and Gavin Newsom would levy a one-time 5% tax on residents worth over $1bn A controversial proposal in California to impose a wealth tax on billionaires has gained enough signatures to qualify for the ballot in November, state officials announced on Wednesday. The news is set to intensify an already heated debate around the tax, which has pitted tech moguls and the state’s governor, Gavin Newsom, against the labor unions backing the measure. Continue reading

stories.title: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations
stories.summary: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countries Barbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement. Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the traffick

stories.title: Pete Hegseth accuses Nato countries of ‘free riding’ in combative address
stories.summary: US defence secretary addresses allies in latest attempt to get Europe to raise military budgets Pete Hegseth has announced a review of the US military presence across Europe, in a combative address to Nato allies where he threatened to cut force numbers in countries spending the least on defence. The US defence secretary, speaking at a meeting of Nato defence ministers in Brussels, accused some countries of “free riding” and others of being shameful for not allowing their airbases to be used by

stories.title: Star-studded ceremony welcomes Obama Presidential Center to Chicago – live
stories.summary: Former presidents, heads of state and celebrities converged on a lakefront park in Chicago’s South Side to dedicate the Obama Presidential Center Sign up for the Breaking News US email He calls the situation a “win-win” for the US. Vance is here, and he starts by claiming that Trump’s peace deal with Iran “is already bearing real fruits for the American people”, with 12.5m barrels going through the strait of Hormuz last night and gas prices dropping below $4 today for the first time since the co

stories.title: Jackie O pursues claim against Kiis FM after Kyle Sandilands settles – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Media union blasts Pauline Hanson’s ‘bitter, unprofessional’ attack on Guardian journalist Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Coalition says it’s good Hanson and One Nation getting the scrutiny of a party on the rise Kevin Hogan, the shadow assistant treasurer, says he thinks it’s good Hanson was able to speak at the press club yesterday as One Nation “needs to be put under much more scrutiny” amid surging support. The GetUp stunt completely back

stories.title: Germany news: Minister weighs cuts to UN contributions
stories.summary: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul has criticized the UN after Germany failed in a recent bid for a Security Council seat. Meanwhile, industrial employment levels in the country are at a decade low. DW has more.

stories.title: NATO: US lashes out, alliance head says everything's fine
stories.summary: US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth threatened NATO allies over lack of spending and tepid support for the war in Iran. Members say they are on the right path but need time as NATO boss Mark Rutte, claims all is good.

stories.title: Oil nations on edge in the face of new climate coalition
stories.summary: Even amid an ongoing energy crunch, major economies are dodging firm timelines to phase out coal, oil and gas. Fossil fuel-producing nations now fear new clean energy alliances could ramp up the pressure to go green.

stories.title: Israel says it's cutting ties to EU's Kaja Kallas
stories.summary: Israel's foreign minister says he is severing contact with the EU's foreign affairs chief Kaja Kallas. Gideon Saar attributed the move to a media report alleging that Kallas compared Israel to apartheid-era South Africa.

stories.title: EU summit: Leaders to discuss Ukraine, global issues
stories.summary: European Union leaders are gathering in Brussels for a two-day summit. The meeting is expected to focus on supporting Ukraine, the EU’s next long-term budget, and addressing global economic challenges. DW has more.

stories.title: Mass protest by ultra-Orthodox Jews in Israel over army conscription
stories.summary: Thousands of ultra-Orthodox Jews protest near Netanya against mandatory army service, demanding release of draft refusers amid Israel’s wartime strains.

stories.title: Video: Hundreds of drones target Russia in major Ukrainian attack
stories.summary: Russian officials said Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks of the war. Images and video released by the Russian media showed massive fires raging at the Moscow Oil Refinery, located about 15 kilometers from the Kremlin. Thick black clouds of smoke rose over the city.

stories.title: Lawyer in high-profile Ugandan treason case charged with related offence
stories.summary: Uganda's military chief had boasted of "the hurt and pain" he would inflict on Erias Lukwago, who was arrested earlier this week.

stories.title: Kaja Kallas responds after Israeli foreign minister says he will 'sever all contact' with her
stories.summary: Kallas said she valued "dialogue and engagement" with Israel, but she made no mention of the reported apartheid comment.

stories.title: Brussels rally presses EU to curb trade with settlements
stories.summary: Hundreds of demonstrators gathered in Brussels on 17 June ahead of a European Union summit. Protesters call for sanctions on Israel and a ban on goods produced in Israeli settlements.

stories.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
stories.summary: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

stories.title: Humanoid robots and smart homes put AI centre stage at VivaTech 2026 in Paris
stories.summary: France is putting artificial intelligence and robotics in the spotlight at VivaTech 2026, which opened on 17 June at Paris Expo Porte de Versailles.

stories.title: Cuba's President Miguel Díaz-Canel admits 'urgent changes' needed to overcome crisis
stories.summary: The oil blockade imposed by President Donald Trump in January has brought the island's already moribund economy to the brink of collapse, marked by power cuts sometimes lasting over 30 hours.

stories.title: The EU’s secret weapon for enlargement: AI
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission is using artificial intelligence to speed up its work with prospective members of the EU. Two officials from the Commission who work on enlargement told POLITICO their department has been using an AI tool to assess candidate countries’ laws to ensure they align with EU legislation, as staff struggle to cope with an increased workload caused by so many countries banging on the bloc’s door. The AI tool used is the Commission’s own creation, the officia

stories.title: EU lawmakers back suspension of wastewater cleanup rules in right-wing alliance
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Parliament backed a conservative push Thursday to pause parts of the EU’s wastewater cleanup law, over fears that making drugmakers pay to remove micropollutants could fuel medicine shortages. Lawmakers in Strasbourg voted in favor of a proposal to suspend industry’s obligation to pay to remove pollutants such as antibiotics and microplastics from wastewater, in a move that risks putting Europe’s polluter-pays principle in the law on hold. Members of the European Parliame

stories.title: Guardian Australia’s Matilda Boseley wins major award at 2026 Walkley mid-year media prizes
stories.summary: Boseley won for her high-profile, multiplatform political explainer series, Parliamen-Tea: explaining the chaos of Australian politics Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Guardian Australia’s Matilda Boseley has taken out one of the top honours at the 2026 Walkley mid-year media prizes, winning the award for innovative storytelling. stories.title: Nigel Farage to join populist and rightwing figures at ‘anti-woke Davos’ in London
stories.summary: Exclusive: Event co-founded by Jordan Peterson will bring together rightwing figures, US state officials and anti-abortionists in London Nigel Farage and fellow Reform UK MPs Sarah Pochin and Andrew Rosindell will be there. As will a plethora of Reform advisers, backroom staff and figures, such as Ben Delo, a British crypto billionaire who has given £4m to Nigel Farage’s party . Yet as populist-right politicians from across the globe and their multimillionaire backers prepare for this year’s All

stories.title: The CGT ‘backflip’ is more tweak than transformation. Labor hasn’t changed its mind on housing
stories.summary: Do the concessions undermine the original objective of helping young Australians buy their own home? No Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The noise, negative headlines and internet memes that have surrounded Labor’s capital gains tax changes since their budget night unveiling made a backdown feel like an inevitability. It wasn’t a question of if concessions would be offered but rather when and, most importantly, how much they might undermine the original purpose. Conti

stories.title: Iran peace deal makes clear how far US has been forced to retreat since 2025
stories.summary: Plan is admission US could not achieve what it sought through war as red line after red line has been erased Only a man with an unparalleled ignorance of history such as Donald Trump would have signed America’s peace treaty with Iran at Versailles, the byword for national humiliation. And only a man with an impish sense of humour such as Emmanuel Macron would have suggested it. It is easy to cast Trump in the role of the humiliated and hurt German count Ulrich von Brockdorff-Rantzau. The treaty

stories.title: South African men sentenced in ‘world’s largest’ rhino horn trafficking case
stories.summary: ‘Mastermind’ Dawie Groenewald given fine of 2m rand or four-year jail term almost 16 years after arrest Two traffickers of rhino horns have been sentenced by a South African court in what police said was the world’s largest such case, partly bringing to an end an almost two-decade legal saga. Dawie Groenewald and Tielman Erasmus had faced more than 1,700 charges ranging from illegally hunting and dehorning rhinos to racketeering and money laundering. Continue reading...

stories.title: Thousands of Knicks fans celebrate big win with joyous New York parade: ‘We family now’
stories.summary: Parents and their kids, new and old fans and a few celebrities gathered to honor the team’s NBA Championship Thousands of Knicks fans – decked out in blue and orange jerseys, shorts, hats, necklaces and more – gathered in downtown New York City on Thursday to celebrate the team’s NBA championship in a lively ticker-tape parade. All along Church Street, the street running parallel to the parade route, fans lit joints, threw back shots of Fireball whiskey and drank Coronas, within view of bemused

stories.title: Iran announces plans to bring in maritime fees for strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Tehran says fees to cover cost of managing waterway will come into effect at end of 60-day negotiation period Iran has announced plans to introduce a system of maritime fees in the strait of Hormuz in two months, after the 60-day period of negotiation that has been triggered by the signing of the memorandum of understanding. Tehran, claiming a historic victory over the US, said the strait was under its control and a European plan for a naval mission to escort ships though the strait would not be

stories.title: ‘Cynical to get power’: Michel Barnier on Boris Johnson, Brexit and the EU’s future
stories.summary: Former negotiator believes in an unstable world, it is ‘perfectly possible’ the UK can rejoin the EU with old opt-outs UK could keep special pre-Brexit terms if it rejoined EU, Michel Barnier says A couple of years ago, Michel Barnier spent a weekend with Boris Johnson’s father, Stanley. It was not some ghoulish Brexit spin-off of The Traitors, but the result of the former EU negotiator’s wife, Isabelle, being a close friend of Johnson’s French cousin, Anne du Boucheron, the owner of Château de

stories.title: Teenager wrongly arrested wins race discrimination claim against the Met
stories.summary: Daryl McLune was 16 when he was held for 23 hours on suspicion of attempting to murder his mother after she tried to take her own life A teenager who was wrongly arrested for the attempted murder of his mother minutes after she had tried to kill herself has won a race discrimination claim against the Metropolitan police. A jury found that the Met discriminated against Daryl McLune, who was 16 at the time, because he was black. Continue reading...

stories.title: BBC pulls Ashley Cain documentary over abusive and misogynistic remarks
stories.summary: BBC says vetting process ‘clearly failed’ after Guardian reveals presenter’s past comments about women Warning: this article contains sexually explicit, offensive language The BBC has pulled a documentary series with its controversial presenter Ashley Cain after revelations over his history of abusive and misogynistic comments about women. In a statement late on Thursday, the BBC said its vetting requirements had “clearly failed” in the case of Cain, who was lauded by executives at the corporati

stories.title: Democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George to be next mayor of Washington DC
stories.summary: City councillor who ran on expanding childcare, education and housing slated for office after opponent concedes Democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George is slated to be the next mayor of Washington DC after her opponent conceded on Thursday. Lewis George, a city council member, ran on a platform of expanding childcare, education and housing, and revoking the district’s cooperation with federal immigration enforcement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump administration reverses decision to scrap ocean monitoring system
stories.summary: Move to dismantle $368m sea observatory initiative faced opposition from experts and lawmakers The Donald Trump administration has reversed its decision to dismantle a $368m deep-sea observation system following an outcry from lawmakers and ocean experts . On Thursday, the National Science Foundation announced that it would halt plans to dismantle the Ocean Observatories Initiative, stating: “effective immediately, [it] will not proceed with further removal or descoping of equipment from the rem

stories.title: Lib Dem MP Cameron Thomas suspended amid police investigation
stories.summary: MP for Tewkesbury understood to have been arrested by Gloucestershire police on Wednesday night A Liberal Democrat MP has had the whip and membership of the party suspended after he became the subject of a police investigation. Cameron Thomas, the MP for Tewkesbury, was arrested by Gloucestershire police on Wednesday night, it is understood. Continue reading...

stories.title: Rejoining customs union would not fix damage caused by Brexit, research finds
stories.summary: Exclusive: Economists find Brexit caused 12% depression in UK exports, most of which is due to leaving single market Brexit has depressed UK exports to the EU by 12%, and rejoining the customs union would undo only a fraction of the damage, research shared with the Guardian shows. With the UK’s future relationship with the bloc likely to feature prominently in a potential Labour leadership contest, the economists John Springford and Anton Spisak, of the Centre for European Reform, provide fresh

stories.title: Norway’s monarchy once seemed like a fairytale – recent crises have exposed its dark underbelly | Magnus Nome
stories.summary: The royals were always popular, but Crown Princess Mette-Marit’s Epstein connections and her son’s rape convictions have profoundly shaken public trust The Norwegian monarchy is in crisis. Not because its future queen is gravely ill , nor even because her son has this week been convicted of serious crimes , but because the institution’s greatest asset – public trust – has been eroded by a series of self-inflicted mistakes. Yesterday, it was announced that Norway’s crown princess, Mette-Marit, un

stories.title: Cuba approves economic reforms amid US pressure
stories.summary: Cuba's Communist Party has signed off on a package of free market reforms. One member called the changes a response to "economic war," while the party said they were not a "deviation" from the socialist project.

stories.summary: A strong option at left back is overdue for Germany. Nathaniel Brown is quickly becoming a star at the World Cup.

stories.title: Niger security forces battle gunmen at airport
stories.summary: Niger's security forces engaged in an hours-long shootout with gunmen at the nation's main international airport. No one has taken responsibility but Islamists are suspected of being behind the attack.

stories.summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

stories.summary: Following the tentative deal with the US, Iran is planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The German Bundeswehr is planning to assist with the mine-clearing.

An international team analysed the dramatic shift in policies on cannabis between 2000 and 2025, including how the numbers of people taking the drug,

stories.title: Dubai property sales have fallen ‘off a cliff’ since start of Middle East war
stories.summary: Sellers of luxury villas have wiped tens of millions of pounds off asking prices, with sales down 19% in May from the previous month Property sales in Dubai have fallen “off a cliff”, a leading market watcher has said, after war in the Middle East forced a dramatic slowdown in one of the world’s most expensive real estate markets. Sales in the city dropped 19% in May compared with the previous month, accelerating from a 4% drop in April, the researcher ValuStrat found. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Annak ellenére, hogy korábban arról volt szó: az amerikai elnök bálterme kizárólag magánadományokból épül majd fel.

stories.title: Jair Bolsonaro’s son sentenced to four years in jail for seeking US interference in father’s Brazil coup trial
stories.summary: Brazil supreme court finds that Eduardo Bolsonaro – who resides in the US - tried to get sanctions put on judges trying ex-president over coup plot Brazil’s supreme court has sentenced Eduardo Bolsonaro to four years and two months in prison after finding him guilty of courting US ⁠interference in his father’s coup plot trial last year. The office of Brazil’s ‌prosecutor general had ‌charged Eduardo Bolsonaro – who lives in the US - courting interference from the Trump administration to help Jai

stories.title: Seven-year-old Abdiqadir was hit in a US airstrike. Without a $750 operation, he may lose his ability to walk
stories.summary: Abdiqadir Salah was pierced by shrapnel in a bombing that killed 12 in Somalia. But as the US denies civilians were hurt they face no hope of compensation Read more: Killed walking home from school: why did Somali children become targets of US drone strikes? A seven-year-old boy who was riddled with shrapnel during a deadly US airstrike in Somalia faces losing his ability to walk unless he has a £750 emergency operation. But Abdiqadir Salah’s family cannot afford the surgery and the US – which r

stories.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring
stories.summary: Major operation launched after spate of pet thefts in Ho Chi Minh City, according to local media Police in Vietnam have rescued more than 400 cats in a bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports. More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Period tax’ on sanitary products to be abolished, says Pakistan minister
stories.summary: Campaigners welcome announcement cutting levies on menstrual health items, but say their work to end period poverty is ‘far from over’ Pakistan plans to abolish “period tax”, in a victory for young campaigners who had taken the government to court over the charges. Finance minister Muhammad Aurangzeb announced that sanitary towels and related items were “daily necessities that are indispensable for women’s health, dignity and full participation in social activities”, and said he intended to remo

stories.title: Protesters to rally against World Cup sponsor Hyundai before Mexico game
stories.summary: Focus on business dealings with mining company Guadalajara rally to highlight fate of ‘disappeared’ Hyundai will be targeted by protesters at a rally before the Group A game between Mexico and South Korea in Guadalajara on Thursday, due to the World Cup sponsor’s business dealings with the South American mining company Ternium. A 2025 report from the environmental group Mighty Earth criticised Hyundai’s involvement in what they described as a “dirty steel supply chain”, as the South Korean motor

stories.title: Starmer warns against ‘looking backwards’ to Brexit after rivals back UK’s return to EU
stories.summary: Speaking at the G7 summit, prime minister says he stands by Labour’s manifesto pledge not to rejoin bloc The UK and the European Union should not waste time “looking backwards” to Brexit, Keir Starmer said on Wednesday, as he comes under pressure to reconsider rejoining the EU. The prime minister reaffirmed his government’s manifesto commitment to not re-enter the bloc, but said there had been “real progress” with the relationship, which was “slowly but surely building”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Bavarian top court rules state spy agency can monitor AfD
stories.summary: Bavaria's regional domestic intelligence agency can monitor the right-wing populist AfD, the state's top court has ruled. Several states are trying to implement similar steps, with the opposition party challenging it.

stories.title: FBI foils plot targeting White House UFC event, five arrested across multiple states
stories.summary: Five suspects from Ohio, Missouri and California are in custody after the FBI learned of the threat four days before the South Lawn event.

stories.title: 'They came with machetes' - deadline looms for migrants to leave South Africa
stories.summary: Protesters have set 30 June as the date for all undocumented migrants to leave the country.

stories.title: From regime change to regime deal? How Tehran's political camps are reacting to leaked US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Iran's hardliners have framed the framework deal as a victory, even as Trump dismissed reports of a $300bn reconstruction fund and warned he would resume strikes if Tehran did not comply.

stories.title: Luigi Mangione to use psychiatric defence in health CEO murder trial, judge says
stories.summary: Brian Thompson was killed in December 2024 as he walked to a Manhattan hotel for UnitedHealth Group’s annual investor conference.

stories.title: Labour came to power with no big idea for relations with EU, says former top diplomat
stories.summary: Ivan Rogers, Britain’s EU ambassador from 2013 to 2017, says party’s ideas did not ‘remotely measure up’ to challenge Labour arrived in power with no big idea on the UK’s future relationship with the EU, a former British ambassador to Brussels has said. Ivan Rogers, Britain’s EU ambassador from 2013 to 2017, said Labour presented “a ragbag of issues” on the EU in its manifesto, which did not “remotely measure up to the challenge of the times” and would “make no measurable difference to the UK ma

stories.title: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
stories.summary: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese culture The activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar. The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a section of the wall near Beijing. Continue read

stories.title: Abandoned pony grows his strength in fourth floor flat – video
stories.summary: When Mile the pony was rejected by his mother at birth, his owner, Andjelka Josipovic, decided to take him in. Each night she carries him up to her fourth-floor flat in the Croatian town of Rovinj. Mile spends his days on a ranch but needs regular feeding and attention during the night. Josipovic hopes that after a few weeks he will be strong enough to stay on the ranch full time Continue reading...

stories.title: Why did Russian warship fire warning shots in the Channel? – The Latest
stories.summary: Keir Starmer has called the firing of warning shots by a Russian warship at a British yacht sailing across the Channel on Tuesday ‘deeply concerning and reckless’. Russia’s defence ministry said the yacht was on a ‘dangerous course’ and several attempts were made to contact it – a claim disputed by the retired couple onboard the 40ft yacht. Nosheen Iqbal speaks to the Guardian’s defence and security editor, Dan Sabbagh Continue reading...

stories.title: Titan sub: design flaws and company groupthink central to catastrophe, report finds
stories.summary: Canadian officials find structural defects in material used for hull and say firm failed to fully test ‘novel’ design Canadian safety officials have issued a damning report on the catastrophic final voyage of the Titan submersible, finding that the US company behind the expedition was overcome by “groupthink” and “confirmation bias” and failed to understand the profound risks confronting their largely untested craft. The 6.7-metre (22ft) carbon fibre submersible dipped below the surface of the A

stories.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
stories.summary: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

stories.title: Ebola and the US patient spotlight global health 'injustice'
stories.summary: The story of the US doctor who survived Ebola with experimental treatment in Germany highlights wider inequities in global health. It also reveals how sharply medical standards can differ between African countries.

stories.title: CRISPR gene-editing for crops: Precision tool or new risk?
stories.summary: The EU has eased restrictions on crops developed using novel genetic techniques. While supporters say the technology could help farmers adapt to climate change, critics argue its effects remain poorly understood.

stories.title: Germany: Reports of antisemitism in 2025 remain high
stories.summary: The Federal Association of Departments for Research and Information on Antisemitism (RIAS) recorded over 8,700 cases they classify as antisemitic hostility in 2025, many of which were connected with the State of Israel.

stories.title: UN chief visits Haiti as gang violence soars and number of displaced hits 1.5 million
stories.summary: Guterres’ first stop was the headquarters of the new gang-suppression force, which the UN Security Council approved in September.

stories.title: Kazakhstan’s skies welcome Central Asia’s first Paragliding World Cup
stories.summary: Around 150 pilots from 33 countries have gathered in Kazakhstan's Zhetysu region for Central Asia's first-ever Paragliding World Cup.

stories.title: Iranian oil tankers pass US blockade ahead of peace deal signing ceremony
stories.summary: The naval blockade began on 13 April and targeted all vessels entering or departing Iranian ports and coastal areas.

stories.title: Washington's Lincoln Memorial pool turns green days after renovation
stories.summary: An algae bloom has turned Washington's newly renovated Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool green just days after a $14 million makeover.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russian drone attack on Zaporizhzhia home kills one, injures seven
stories.summary: At least one person was killed and seven others injured after a Russian drone struck a residential building in the southeastern Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia on 16 June, sparking a major fire and causing extensive damage, Ukrainian officials said.

stories.title: Javier Bardem joins Hollywood greats at landmark ceremony
stories.summary: Spanish film star Javier Bardem was honoured in Los Angeles on 16 June with a place in the celebrated forecourt of the TCL Chinese Theatre, a distinction reserved for some of cinema's biggest names.

stories.title: Status quo at Jerusalem's holiest site under threat as Israeli nationalists flout rules
stories.summary: Israeli nationalists are increasingly flouting a convention on how faiths share the al-Aqsa mosque compound.

stories.title: Thousands flock to Art Basel in Switzerland as market outlook improves
stories.summary: Art Basel 2026 in Basel draws thousands as high-end works by Picasso, Warhol and Hockney fuel a recovering global art market after years of decline.

stories.title: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon despite Trump criticism
stories.summary: Speaking on Tuesday, Trump said Israel's PM Benjamin Netanyahu needed "to be more responsible with respect to Lebanon".

stories.title: Norway's crown princess undergoes successful lung transplant, palace says
stories.summary: Crown Princess Mette-Marit, who suffered from pulmonary fibrosis, will now spend several weeks recovering in hospital.

stories.title: Hundreds of cats rescued from being eaten in Vietnam
stories.summary: More than 500 cats were seized in Vietnam after police dismantled a cat meat trafficking network, with dozens of animals later reunited with their owners.
